rules: clients are not the control plane
workers are adapters behind a governed contract
terminal is a governed tool surface, not the primary UX
raw model invoke and managed worker execution stay separate

recommended_milestones.name: session_task_worker_contract
recommended_milestones.goal: define the stable control-plane contract that all future clients and workers must use
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: one stable control-plane contract exists that chat and IDE clients can use without special-case behavior

recommended_milestones.name: operator_chat_surface
recommended_milestones.goal: build the operator-first chat surface on top of the shared control-plane contract
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: non-technical operators can run governed sessions without using terminal or IDE surfaces

recommended_milestones.name: worker_adapter_pack
recommended_milestones.goal: add managed worker execution behind the control-plane contract
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: at least one managed worker can execute tasks through the session contract with approval, audit, and evidence capture

recommended_milestones.name: ide_and_enterprise_ingress_pack
recommended_milestones.goal: extend the same governed system to developer and enterprise ingress surfaces
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: developers and non-developers can enter the same governed system through different surfaces with a shared audit and evidence model

recommended_milestones.name: enterprise_hardening_and_rollout_pack
recommended_milestones.goal: close the enterprise deployment posture gaps after client and worker surfaces exist
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: Fortune 500 deployment posture is supported across UI, IDE, API, and worker surfaces with clear governance boundaries

recommended_immediate_next_step: author the M16 control-plane contract spec and entity/event model before building chat, Codex worker control, VS Code, or ticket/chatops ingress
parallel_execution_note: M16 architecture/spec work may begin while M15 remains blocked on Windows execution-host proof, but M15 must not be marked complete until Phase D and final launch sign-off are closed.
